reviews.review_msg: I really enjoyed COGS100 with Margaret Grant. Yes, there is a lot of course content to cover and there are a few readings each week, but if you engage with the material it is straightforward and enjoyable. I liked this format of hybrid teaching. Lectures were great. Assignments and tests not too difficult. Highly recommend taking a course with her.

The problem was that Margaret specializes in linguistics, so the content tended to be more about the linguistic side of cognitive science. I'm not sure how much she really knew about the other topics in cognitive science but I was still able to learn a lot. She also seemed a bit unorganized at times but she was caring towards the students.

If you appreciate your peace then do NOT take this class. His marking is completely unreasonable and the grading scale is unfair. He is a funny prof but is not worth taking the class for. I would steer clear of this class and prof.

His lectures are really hard to follow as he doesn't post slides, the slides have little to no info on them, and the slides do not correlate to the topic point he is teaching. They also do not follow the list that is on canvas, so it is hard to follow, and you have to guess what topic he is actually teaching. He does not grade fair either.

PLEASE DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU LOVE UR MENTAL HEALTH. his tests are totally rigged, with multiple choice answers THAT ARE ALL RIGHT BUT YOU STILL GET THE ANSWER WRONG.  I took this class with an open mindset, but that quickly shut down.

Honestly, it's really hard to understand her and she can be very very unclear. But I got my first A+ from her because I know how her class works. Really, you just have to write as much as you can about whatever the topic is and show that you are really interested and really want to do well. Then the grade just comes.
